solution.p: We will factor 4y^{2}+28yt+49t^{2} where a=4, b=28, and c=49.

Perfect Square Formula: The formula for perfect square trinomials is (a \pm b)^2=a^2 \pm 2ab + b^2. Note that the polynomial 4y^{2}+28yt+49t^{2} matches the formula's right side, and we have: \begin{aligned}[t]4y^{2}+28yt+49t^{2}\amp =\left(2y\right)^2+2\left(2y\right)\left(7t\right)+\left(7t\right)^2\newline \amp =\left(2y+7t\right)\mathopen{}\left(2y+7t\right)\newline \amp =\left(2y+7t\right)^{2}\end{aligned}
AC Method: In this method, we first calculate the product of a and c in ax^2+bx+c. For this problem, we have (4) \cdot (49) = 196.

The number 196 can be factored into the product of two numbers in the following ways:  egin{array}{lll}1(196)\amp -1(-196)\amp 2(98)\amp -2(-98)
ewline4(49)\amp -4(-49)\amp 7(28)\amp -7(-28)
ewline14(14)\amp -14(-14)\amp \end{array} Looking at the corresponding sums, we want a sum of 28, and this sum happens with 14+14=28 So we write  egin{aligned}[t]4y^{2}+28yt+49t^{2}\amp =4y^{2}+14yt+14yt+49t^{2}
ewline \amp =2y\mathopen{}\left(2y+7tight)+7t\mathopen{}\left(2y+7tight)
ewline \amp =\left(2y+7tight)\mathopen{}\left(2y+7tight)
ewline \amp =\left(2y+7tight)^{2}\end{aligned}Generic Rectangles Method: First, we set up generic rectangles by putting the first term and third term in 4y^{2}+28yt+49t^{2} into the top left and bottom right rectangles:
The first term's coefficient, 4, can be factored into the product of two numbers (where the first factor is positive) in the following ways: \begin{array}{llll}1(4)\amp 2(2)\amp \end{array}
The third term's coefficient, 49, can be factored into the product of two numbers in the following ways: \begin{array}{llll}1(49)\amp -1(-49)\amp 7(7)\amp -7(-7)\newline\end{array}

We put each pair into the corresponding places next to those generic rectangles, and try to match the area of those rectangles with 4y^{2}+28yt+49t^{2} by guess-and-check. The following generic rectangles show the solution:

Dimensions of those generic rectangles in the diagram give us the solution: \begin{aligned}[t]4y^{2}+28yt+49t^{2}\amp =\left(2y+7t\right)\mathopen{}\left(2y+7t\right)\newline \amp =\left(2y+7t\right)^{2}\end{aligned}
